evidence: The 'Double yellow solid center line' is a standard road marking used in the Philippines to denote no-overtaking zones.
The 'White painted curb edge' is frequently observed in Philippine road infrastructure, especially near settlements or improved rural roads.
The combination of 'Palm tree' (pinnate type) and 'Reddish/lateritic soil' is highly characteristic of the tropical rural landscape in the Philippines.
A white car with a 'yellow ribbon' or tape on the roof rail is a well-known meta-clue for Google Street View coverage in the Philippines.
Driving on the right side of the road matches the Philippines' traffic regulations.
contradictions: Double yellow lines are also common in North and South America, but the specific 'yellow ribbon' meta-clue is distinctively associated with the Philippines in the context of Google Street View.
Thailand and Indonesia have similar vegetation and soil but drive on the left.
